<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The global graph will center on a high-degree hub node.

The "All" filter currently hides non-neighbor nodes, leaving only the hub's immediate connections visible.

The current "All" filter likely hides too many nodes. A radial layout would place the focal note at the center, with direct neighbors in an inner ring and indirect links farther out.

The radial layout may apply to the local graph on individual note pages rather than only the global view. Each node would be assigned a hop distance from the focal note via BFS. Nodes would then be placed at concentric radii based on their hop count.

Link distances will vary by hop: edges to the focal note stay shorter than same-hop edges. A radial force will pull each node toward its target radius. The focal note stays fixed at the center while the generic center force is reduced for other nodes. The visibility logic still needs to be reconciled with this layout.

The radial layout probably targets the local graph, where the focal note is the current page and all visible nodes are direct neighbors. The force simulation currently scatters these nodes randomly instead of keeping them in a tight inner ring. On the global graph, the "All" filter is already limited to the hub and its direct neighbors, so the radial placement would apply to that smaller subset.

A BFS function will compute hop distances from the focal node. Initial positions will place nodes in radial rings by hop count. The simulation will add a radial constraint force and shorten link distances for edges touching the focal note. The focal note must stay centered during initialization to prevent drift.

The "All notes" filter should display the full graph, so viewNodes will be updated to show all nodes rather than only immediate neighbors. On the global graph, nodes with a direct link to the focal note will sit closer to the center, while indirect nodes will fall farther out.

I will leave viewNodes unchanged since the user did not ask to show all nodes. I am now writing the BFS helper that assigns each node a hop count from the focal note.

The BFS helper now marks disconnected nodes as outermost. The radial layout starts with a base radius for the first hop and calculates step sizes between rings.

The focal note is locked to the canvas center. Nodes are grouped by hop count, then distributed evenly around each ring radius.

The simulation applies a radial force to each non-focus node. It pulls the node toward its target ring radius using a scaled directional vector.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Implementing a radial hop-based layout. The focal note will sit at the center. Notes with direct links will cluster closer to the center.

Adding a BFS function to compute hop distances from the focus node. Nodes will be placed on concentric rings by hop count. Simulation forces will pull nodes toward their target radii based on hop distance.

Nodes outside the focus node's connected component are assigned the maximum hop distance. Ring radii scale from 14% to 27% of the smaller canvas dimension. Simulated link distances will also factor into the hop-based radial layout.

Link distances now scale with hop difference: 65 pixels for direct links to the focus node, 80 plus 25 times the hop gap otherwise, and 100 for disconnected nodes. Radial attraction pulls each node toward its target ring radius, scaled by simulation alpha.

The radial force now pulls each node directly toward its target ring radius. Center attraction is reduced to 0.001 when hop distances are active, since the radial layout already pulls nodes inward. Initial positions will place nodes evenly spaced on each ring by hop count.

Nodes will be grouped by hop count and spread evenly across each ring. The view filter currently hides distant neighbors when a focus node is active; this will be adjusted to show all nodes on the global graph.
</details></think>

Implementing hop-based radial layout: focal note pinned at center, direct neighbors on an inner ring, and farther hops on outer rings. Updating `viewNodes` so the global graph shows all notes, not only first-hop neighbors.

_**Agent (default)**_

The graph layout now uses a **radial, hop-based placement** centered on the focal note.

**Layout**
- The focal note stays pinned at the center.
- **Direct links (1 hop)** sit on the inner ring, closest to center.
- **2-hop, 3-hop, …** notes sit on progressively outer rings.
- Disconnected notes go on the outermost ring.

**Forces**
- Shorter link distances for edges touching the focal note.
- A radial force keeps each node near its hop-based ring during simulation.
- The generic center pull is reduced so rings stay readable instead of collapsing inward.

**Visibility**
- The global graph “All” filter now shows **every note** (not just first-hop neighbors), so you can see the full depth layout around the hub.

This applies to both the full graph page and the local graph on individual notes. With `npm run watch` running, refresh `/notes/graph/` or any note with a local graph to see it. Use **Reset** if a graph was already open and still looks like the old layout.
